page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, maintaining a strict one thread per core ratio to ensure dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading overhead.

This instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per CPU core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data deployment. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads that require consistent, non-shared CPU performance. It is suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics nodes, and lightweight computational tasks where predictable execution times are prioritized over high memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance provides 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It contains no local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. With dedicated CPU allocation and a single thread per core configuration, this instance delivers predictable, non-shared processing power. It is designed for compute-intensive workloads, including batch processing, web serving, and distributed compilation, offering a balanced, qualitative cost-to-performance profile for dedicated compute resources.
